topic: trying to figure out accounting and taxes

turns.content: help na ka 😩 I just got a letter from the Revenue Department about taxes and I don't understand ANYTHING. I feel like reading a different language and I speak two languages already haha

Tax letters can be confusing even for people who deal with them regularly! Don't panic. What does it say roughly — is it a filing reminder, a notice about something, or a bill? And have you been filing taxes for your food cart income?

honestly na ka... I never file taxes properly 😅 I just sell food and keep the money. I know that is bad!! but I thought small street vendor don't need to worry about this. now the letter say something about personal income tax and I need to register??

You're not alone — many street food vendors operate informally. But technically, if your annual income exceeds 60,000 baht, you need to file personal income tax under the PND 90 form. You definitely cross that threshold. The good news is that food vendors get a standard 60% deduction on revenue as assumed costs, so you're only taxed on 40% of what you earn. Have you been tracking your income and expenses at all?

arai wa 60,000 baht threshold?! I make more than that in like 4 days 😂 okay so I definitely need to file. I have SOME records in Google Sheets — someone teach me to track daily costs — but I am not very consistent. maybe 4-5 months of data from this year because some nights I am too tired to type numbers

4-5 months of data is actually a decent start — you can use those to estimate the rest. But going forward, my biggest suggestion: consider hiring a freelance accountant. In Bangkok they handle small businesses for about 2,000-3,000 baht per month. They file everything, calculate deductions, handle the Revenue Department paperwork. You just give them your numbers each month.

2,000-3,000 baht per month?? that is like selling 40-50 extra plates na ka. and then I don't have to think about taxes AT ALL?? they just do everything?? because honestly my brain is for cooking not for numbers haha

Pretty much! A good accountant would file your annual return, calculate deductions to minimize what you owe, handle all the paperwork, and tell you what receipts to keep. Some even accept messy Google Sheets and turn them into proper books. The money you save from correct deductions often more than pays their fee. Ask around — other vendors or small business owners near you probably know someone.

okay I will ask my friend who sell som tum near my spot — she seem like she have her life together haha maybe she know an accountant. thank you na ka!! I feel less scared now. I thought they were going to come arrest me or something 😂 goodnight!! 🙏

Haha no arrests! The Revenue Department sends these letters to get people into the system, not to punish them. As long as you start filing properly going forward you'll be fine. Get that referral from your som tum friend and keep filling in your Google Sheet every day — your future accountant will thank you. Good luck with the tax stuff! 🙏

wait one more question na ka — if I hire accountant do I still need to keep all the receipts?? because I lose receipt like every day haha. some vendor give receipt some don't and the paper ones from the market fade in like 2 weeks 😂

Yes, keeping receipts helps a lot — but here's a trick: just snap a photo of every receipt with your phone right when you get it. Make a folder called "receipts" and dump them all in there. That way even if the paper fades, you have the photo. For vendors that don't give receipts, just note down the amount and what you bought in your Google Sheet. Your accountant can work with that.

phone photo!! why I never think of that na ka so simple 😂 okay from tomorrow I take photo of every receipt before it disappear. and I type my no-receipt cost in the Google Sheet. my accountant going to be SO impressed when I show up with organised data haha. okay goodnight for real this time!! 🙏

Haha, an organised Pan is an unstoppable Pan! Your future accountant will love you for it. Start the photo habit tomorrow and it'll be second nature in a week. Goodnight and sweet dreams — no more tax nightmares! 🙏
no more tax nightmares na ka!! from now on I am a responsible tax-paying chicken rice vendor haha 😂 okay goodnight for REAL real this time. sawatdee ka!! 🙏
A responsible tax-paying chicken rice queen! Love it. Goodnight for real real, Pan — tomorrow is a new day of receipts, photos, and perfectly cooked chicken. Sawatdee ka! 🙏
